title: Regularization in Deep Learning
summary: Techniques to prevent overfitting in neural networks.
key_concepts: Dropout: randomly zero activations during training, scale at inference
Batch normalization: normalize layer inputs, learnable γ,β
Layer normalization: normalize across features (used in Transformers)
Weight decay (L2 in Adam is NOT weight decay — use AdamW)
exercises: Implement dropout from scratch (including inference scaling).
Implement batch normalization forward and backward pass from scratch.
Show empirically that BatchNorm speeds up training.
Why is AdamW different from Adam + L2 regularization?
thinking_questions: Why does dropout act as an ensemble method?
Why use Layer Norm instead of Batch Norm in Transformers?
